source_gate.rejection_reason: Official FiscalData DTS API rows expose record_date and fiscal/calendar dimensions, but not a per-record publication timestamp, immutable vintage id, or official release-time field. Because AR-170 required explicit resolution of whether record_date is statement date, publication date, or both before any performance conclusion, the scout stops at the source/vintage gate rather than assuming a lag convention.
sample_observations: The API is official and reachable for table I operating cash balance and table II deposits/withdrawals.

TGA closing balance is available as account_type Treasury General Account (TGA) Closing Balance with src_line_nbr 4; current API stores the value in open_today_bal for that closing-balance row while close_today_bal is the string null.
Operating cash balance filtered rows returned coverage from 2022-04-18 through 2026-06-26 in this check; deposits/withdrawals detail rows returned coverage from 2021-10-01 through 2026-06-26.
No raw DTS or market-data paths were retained.

performance_evaluation.reason_not_run: Source/vintage gate failed before market-data evaluation; no qfa/Alpaca performance metrics were computed or invented.

model_behavior.generate_signals: zero weights for all requested symbols

warnings: Rejecting at source/vintage gate does not imply the DTS economic hypothesis is false; it means this source as accessed did not meet AR-170's vintage proof requirement.
A future source/parser project would need an official publication timestamp or immutable release archive before any DTS/TGA allocation backtest should be scored.
